actions.text: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.
Press a broad paw pad on the stable panel below the horizontal midpoint crease.

actions.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease
actions.text: Sweep the free panel over the horizontal midpoint crease with a slow paw drag to form a valley fold.

actions.target: left midpoint and right midpoint overlap within visual tolerance
actions.text: Use broad visual zones near left midpoint and right midpoint; do not require point-perfect alignment.
Press along the horizontal midpoint crease with repeated gentle paw-pad presses from left midpoint to right midpoint.
Lift the paw vertically so the new valley crease is not dragged out of place.

alignment_target.target: left midpoint and right midpoint overlap within visual tolerance
alignment_target.text: Use broad visual zones near left midpoint and right midpoint; do not require point-perfect alignment.

anchor_grip.text: Press a broad paw pad on the stable panel below the horizontal midpoint crease.

annotations.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease

checks: The new valley crease is visible on the horizontal midpoint crease.
No boundary edge has moved outside the source square.

crease_press.text: Press along the horizontal midpoint crease with repeated gentle paw-pad presses from left midpoint to right midpoint.

landmark_language.center: Use the central visible patch of the paper.
landmark_language.corners: Use large visible corner regions rather than exact points.
landmark_language.crease_line: Use a marked line or raised crease that can be pressed along.
landmark_language.edges: Use the nearest visible boundary as a coarse alignment cue.
landmark_language.midpoint: Treat midpoint as a broad middle region, not a precise point.

executor_profile_definition.name: Cat paw profile
risk_notes: Soft contact makes sharp creases unlikely without a tool or fixture.
Dragging can tear thin paper or shift the whole sheet.

failure_modes: If the paper slides, re-anchor near left midpoint and repeat the fold slowly.
Cat paw profile: Soft contact makes sharp creases unlikely without a tool or fixture.

fold_direction.direction: free panel moves toward the executor over the marked crease
fold_direction.text: Sweep the free panel over the horizontal midpoint crease with a slow paw drag to form a valley fold.

steps.pre_state: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.

release.text: Lift the paw vertically so the new valley crease is not dragged out of place.

steps.title: Add a mast centerline valley

actions.text: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.
Press a broad paw pad on the stable panel below the main diagonal crease.

actions.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease
actions.text: Sweep the free panel over the main diagonal crease with a slow paw drag to form a mountain fold.

actions.target: top-left corner and bottom-right corner overlap within visual tolerance
actions.text: Use broad visual zones near top-left corner and bottom-right corner; do not require point-perfect alignment.
Press along the main diagonal crease with repeated gentle paw-pad presses from top-left corner to bottom-right corner.
Lift the paw vertically so the new mountain crease is not dragged out of place.

alignment_target.target: top-left corner and bottom-right corner overlap within visual tolerance
alignment_target.text: Use broad visual zones near top-left corner and bottom-right corner; do not require point-perfect alignment.

anchor_grip.text: Press a broad paw pad on the stable panel below the main diagonal crease.

annotations.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease

checks: The new mountain crease is visible on the main diagonal crease.
No boundary edge has moved outside the source square.

crease_press.text: Press along the main diagonal crease with repeated gentle paw-pad presses from top-left corner to bottom-right corner.

landmark_language.center: Use the central visible patch of the paper.
landmark_language.corners: Use large visible corner regions rather than exact points.
landmark_language.crease_line: Use a marked line or raised crease that can be pressed along.
landmark_language.edges: Use the nearest visible boundary as a coarse alignment cue.
landmark_language.midpoint: Treat midpoint as a broad middle region, not a precise point.

executor_profile_definition.name: Cat paw profile
risk_notes: Soft contact makes sharp creases unlikely without a tool or fixture.
Dragging can tear thin paper or shift the whole sheet.

failure_modes: If the paper slides, re-anchor near top-left corner and repeat the fold slowly.
Cat paw profile: Soft contact makes sharp creases unlikely without a tool or fixture.

fold_direction.direction: free panel moves away from the executor over the marked crease
fold_direction.text: Sweep the free panel over the main diagonal crease with a slow paw drag to form a mountain fold.

steps.pre_state: Paper lies flat with the target face up and the reference square visible.

release.text: Lift the paw vertically so the new mountain crease is not dragged out of place.

steps.title: Tilt hull diagonal
